title: malus leiocalyca
intro: Malus leiocalyca, also known as the Chinese crabapple, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's bark, leaves, and fruit are used to make various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and promoting cardiovascular health. Additionally, Malus leiocalyca is also used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties.
